channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: It has begun — 25 replies — 100.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
It has begun

Open thread • Replies: 25 • Updated: 2026-06-15 13:33 UTC

OP

SpaceX pre-market starts with UP movement

Early replies (first 3)

62369167
good

i own one share of spcx

62369204
+5% LOCKED IN

169-170$ in pre-market
up from 160$ at friday close

62369215
>>62369156
Am i the only one concerned with all this speculation over an asset that has little intrinsic value?
Everybody has their eyes in this shit. Something else must be the thing to buy right now.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62369217
Ny mother said to get things done you better not mess with Major Tom

62369223
>>62369215
xAI, starlink, spaceX in one stock

if you dont buy you are retarded

62369252
>>62369223
-5 Billions loss in 2025. Datacenters in space?
They are trying to sell this company, doesnt that make you a bit suspicious?

62369264
>>62369156
bitcoin 2.0, another money extraction scam

62369295
>>62369264
ok, faggot boomer

62369373
>>62369252
Every mainstream media outlet is shilling this thing hard and yet anons are walking right into the trap like deers in headlights.

62369439
Ask yourself how many times you'd be willing to play russian roulette with 1 bullet and 100 chambers.

62369442
>>62369439
99 times

62369447
>>62369442

62369548
>>62369373
They're shilling it because indices have to buy a total combined 250 million shares (40% float) over the upcoming weeks. The more they can pump it, the more money they make.

62369568
>>62369156
I'm never touching it

62369572
>>62369156
>just kidding goy, back to work now!

62369613
>>62369572
good I'm still accumulating

62369620
>>62369223
TSLA soon

62369632
>>62369613
same I hope it drops back into the 150s at least, so I can buy with leverage when SPCH is open to buy

62369635
>>62369613
STOP BUYING YOU MODERBITCH!! WHY DONT YOU UNDERTAND SIR? SPCX IS OVERVALUED SIR. SIR YOU NEED TO SHORT, NOT BUY SIR!!

62369672
>>62369613
Sure you are. Post notarized and audited broker statements pronto.

62369679
>>62369672

62369688
>>62369679
That's proof you goofed into the IPO alloc, not proof that you've bought a single share since then. Notarized and audited, btw. You're just milking your bags.

62369703
>>62369688
>instant profit
>goofed
stay poor

62369735
>>62369703
>still accumulating
We still haven't seen where you scooped the remainder of those 1000 shares you asked for.
>notarized
and
>audited

62369981
>>62369156
>>62369735
>>62369703
LIFTOFF!
173 $

item.title: how much money do u need to date women? Also are they ok if u are not ambitious career driven like if i have 10M but NEE… — 52 replies — 88.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
how much money do u need to date women? Also are they ok if u are not ambitious career driven like if i have 10M but NEE…

Open thread • Replies: 52 • Updated: 2026-06-15 12:58 UTC

OP

how much money do u need to date women? Also are they ok if u are not ambitious career driven like if i have 10M but NEET would they be fine with it.. probably not i think they want you to be grinding your job 24/7 and making like 400k a year

Early replies (first 3)

62365550
dating is outdated chud, find something else to focus on

62365555
>>62365550
but i want a gf to watch mobis with, to eat some delicious food, cook together maybe and stuff.. I got a bluray player recently and im building up a collection we could do it together if i had a gf

:(

62365557
>>62365540
a good prostitute in euro cuck land will service you starting from 100$ for 30 mins. Anything else is cope

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62365953
>>62365882
>mean, slobs, irresponsible
These are the complaints women have about the men they continue to date. So the stuff that is annoying but not a dealbreaker.

62365958
>>62365882
yet somehow that is the type they are attracted to and want to have kids with

62365989
>>62365540
>6’5’’
>8 inch sick with 6 inch girth
>230lbs lean and cut muscle but you can’t be a workout freak you need to have the muscle naturally without doing much exercise all the time
>Perfect hairline, as good as a teenagers
>square jaw line
>wide shoulders
>deep voice
>be naturally extroverted and charismatic
>have many high status friends
>have many ex girl friends so you pass women’s pre-selection requirement
>have 10 million dollars but also have a high status job like a brain surgeon or fighter jet pilot so she can brag to her friends what you do for a living
>be able to speak 3 different languages so you can make her wet when your at the high end French restaurant and talk to the waiter in French and order a high end wine bottle
>have a dark and mysterious secret about yourself to keep her intrigued and excited (maybe you are the world’s best assassin, maybe you are an associate of a multi-billion dollar drug operation as a high ranking middleman, or you are a genius and secretly working on a project to save the energy crisis. Something that is cool and will make her wet when she finds out


This is what you need to get a 5/10 or higher in the year of 2020+6

62366005
>>62365587
>>62365738
Do the math OP.

62366027
>>62365989
>have all this
>become her bf
>she cheats on you with a nigga that has NOTHING of this

62366041
>>62365608
Animal should be allowed to just breed and leave, there is nothing like third parties in the interaction.

62366052
>>62365557
>dude just get herpes with a romanian gypsy in a shitty hotel in the suburbs

kys

62366054
>Women or leveraged, margined silver miners?
Mmmmm...... you're gonna have to get back to me on that bro

62366257
>>62365587
>>62365738
>>62366005
^ This system is outdated, but I've invented a new one. The equation is identical, but the compounding effects of the numerator have been upscaled, personality remains the same. Proximity now has a negative and positive value.

62366581
>>62366052
Yep, sounds incredibly based

62366590
>>62365989
Delusion
Just look at families walking around

62366599
>>62365958
>>62365953
Do you know any married men
And those things can be deal breakers, there are lines/limits

62366630
>>62365540
the only girls that care about careers in 2026 are egg freezing hags. you need just enough money to get to where women are (like the bar or anywhere else) and live somewhere. anything past that is diminishing returns.

62366635
anon don't listen to desperate insecure virgins for dating advice
the world is full of poor women who will date you if you have a good personality
when my wife and I started dating, I was literally unemployed and living with my parents
Now I make more than her but it took me 5 years to get there.

62366639
>>62365555
>I got a bluray player recently and im building up a collection

Dad?

62366677
>>62365608
all of these women made a different man spend thousands and wait months before fucking really makes you ponder

62366728
>>62366599
well eventually these kinds of women also tend to be single mothers

62367007
>>62365897
Yes paradoxically the girls that are most wife material aren't the ones going around looking to date. I think if you are out of school you're out of luck
>Girlfriend through connections
I know this sounds stupid, but I'd feel like a cuck if I met my girlfriend through a mutual.
Think about it, MY girlfriend would have been interested in talking to someone else FIRST. I wouldn't want to date a girl with guy friends anyways

Impossible propositions are abound

62367017
>>62366635
Do the math.

62367050
>>62367007
Yeah, that’s pretty retarded.
There are plenty of non-sexual reasons why a guy might know some other girl, like a family member, girlfriend/wife’s friend, former co-worker, former school friend.
You could make friends with girls to try to date one of their friends too.
The crux is on your ability to be a normal person to any normal person regardless of gender or age.
That’s how you find girls who aren’t sluts and divorce rapists. Not how you sleep around though.
So you have to figure out if what you really want is someone to have sex with regularly, or someone to start a family with. Because it’s a lot of social effort.

62367055
>>62367007
I just realized, you may have severely low self esteem if you can’t handle the thought of a gf talking to someone else before you.
Low self esteem is girl repellant
Fix that first

62367080
>>62365555
Checked and damn feels bad, I hope you make it.

>>62365540
Former sex haver here, if she loves you not much. Unironically you could go have a picnic with some bullshit like pizza, or go on a road trip. Strategic use of money is ideal. Don't just buy flowers, get them delivered to her work so all her coworkers get pissed and jealous. You want enough money to lock down a nice wedding and kids but thats really it, this was my fatal mistake. I have enough for three weddings now but opportunities are gone for me.

62367870
I get 1k CAD neetbux and do just fine
If not a demo thread, you're overthinking it retard

62367927
>>62365555
unironically, where are the lazy NEET gfs?

62368381
>>62365555
I just want to watch gun boat ball 2 wit my aspie unicorn believing gf turned housewife mommy with fear of scrutiny. I guess i was born too late

62368990
>>62367050
>>62367055
Think about it though. If a girl has tons of guy friends, that's a pretty clear no signal right?
>Low self esteem
true :(

62369133
>>62368990
>>62368990
You can make friends with women too anon. Women that you aren't interested in can have friends that you are interested in.

Also girls can have guy friends that they don't wanna fuck.

Unironically truely befriend a lesbian, this will teach you how to act around women without acting like a retard as you won't try to fuck her.

Also if you're so insecure talk to a therapist/take shrooms and start working out. Its all ego that you have to let go in the end.

Good luck fren!

62369150
All the matches I got on tinder is 30s post wall roast, I can’t believe the 28yi tinder matches something literally call me old hag when I’m just a 35yo bald, obese, needy, short, no STEM degree, and potentially impotent.

62369226
>>62365540
If you weren't part of the top 10% in 2025, you're lower class

62369807
>>62365540
Elon is worth $1,000,000,000,000 and still can't find a woman that wants to stay with him

item.title: how are they not bankrupt? — 16 replies — 64.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
how are they not bankrupt?

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-06-15 13:23 UTC

OP

>lost the AI race to pretty much everyone else
>OS is becoming so shitty, people are actually switching to Linux
>gaming division has been nuked
>fired whites to hire indians and then fired those indians
what keeps them afloat?

Early replies (first 3)

62369737
>>62369733
The OS being so shitty switching to linux is so true.. I am so fed up with windows if i wasnt addicted to league of legends i would be on linux rn

62369744
>>62369733
They fired the Indians? When? The Redmond area is still swarming with them.

62369750
>>62369737
This imo, the day AutoCad becomes available for Linux natively and vidya gets better support im FUCKING done with Israeli spyware disguised as a American operating system for good.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62369755
>>62369733
They make a shitton of money with Azure and that's not going to change anytime soon, unfortunately.

62369760
>>62369733
If you haven’t figured it out by now because you’re a little bit slow Microsoft is a government sponsored corporation to spy on allies and everyone. Similar to Meta and Google. These aren’t real companies in the sense that you believe them to be. These are state sponsored corporations.

62369762
Linux is still AIDS for most users. You shouldn't get kernal errors just installing Mint on a computer or have to look up console commands to do basic shit or have a choice between a gorillion distros, but that's STILL a thing in the year 2026 and will turn users away.
Their de facto status in business sector will carry them further than many people realise. It doesn't matter how good Libre office is, the turn away from the Office suite will be incredibly slow.
Azure is only just behind AWS for could infrastructure, that's no going anywhere anytime soon.

62369799
>>62369762
How are you this retarded. My god. All of you. Sooo fucking retard. Zorin os has a gaming division and it kicks ass. Every game I own runs on it just fine. If you don't want to use zorin os, just use bottles apps on any linux. It's a windows container system for games and apps without full Linux support. I stopped using windows 11 a long ass time ago.

62369816
>>62369799
>Zorin
literally who lol

62369824
>>62369733
institutional momentum. there's a lot of software that only works on windows, at least reliably so.

62369843
>>62369733
>what keeps them afloat?
idk megalodon institutions and like 90% of all corporations?

62369881
>>62369750
>>62369737
Guaranteed you could run league on some handholding distro right now and not notice a difference. I've got a normie silverlight flavor (bluefin, but they're all basically the same shit) that runs steam games right now no issue, even the non native ones
AutoCAD and other real programs will still probably be a bit buggy though

62369895
>>62369733
Boomer execs

62369900
>>62369733
office365 and enterprise hosting.

The quality is beyond abysmal and I hope they fold.

62369914
>>62369762
Libre office overwrites your files because of an autosave bug on Windows/NTFS (they designed it for Linux's filesystem and refuse ro change it because egotistical FOSS assholes are assholes).
>Just use it on Linux
My assumption is that similar bugs exist that affect Linux builds which will similarly never be fixed. I would never use Libre Office for anything important.

62369921
>>62369733
Doesn't matter how shitty it is, every single business and corpo on earth is forced to use it along with their office/email/meeting software suite.

62369934
>>62369914
Just adding, it's not all FOSS. Blender is a powerhouse, ffmpeg is irreplaceable, SmartTube Next rocks. Great projects contributed to and run by people who actually have the users in mind, and aren't using it just to try to fluff their portfolio or lord over a small digital fiefdom.

item.title: Elon is going to build a data center dAIson sphere around the sun — 34 replies — 44.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Elon is going to build a data center dAIson sphere around the sun

Open thread • Replies: 34 • Updated: 2026-06-15 12:47 UTC

OP

Buy space exploration technologies company stock BUY BUY BUY

Early replies (first 3)

62359811
>>62359800
kek

62359825
>>62359800
will that thing cast a shadow on my backyard? my petunias need the sunlight

62359829
>>62359800
This may be controversial but I think we will see a Dyson Sphere within our lives.

Technology is exponential. Look how long we spent in the Dark Ages with basic mathematics and science then we hit the industrial revolution and in a few hundred of years we are here.

Now with the AI revolution things will happen even faster. Technology enables us to do things faster, better, and more efficiently. With AI we will be travelling between planets regularly in just a few decades.

It might sound crazy but so would a smart phone 200 years ago.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62359837
When is Elon going to build a Mass Relay to the Citadel? I need some blue Asari ass

62359841
>space exploration technologies

62359842
>>62359829
Unless we get real Artificial Superintelligence soon you can forget about that.

62360179
>>62359841
inaneh compootah

62360249
>>62359800
Yes.

62360253
EDS trannies will literally say this is fake, but it's real and it's happening. 10,000 Starship launches per year by 2028, the dyson sphere is happening

62360297
>>62359800
kek

62360306
>>62359800
By 2027 none the less!!!!

62360337
They will build special space radiators of carbon and shit and lasers

62360362
>>62359800
Who's gonna build it? Mexicans?

62360396
>>62359836
its not about beauty, its about turning energy into compute

62360433
>>62359836
How often do you look at the sun

62360446
>>62360362
AI ROBOTS

62360616
>>62359829
Absolutely no chance. A Dyson sphere isn't a single monolithic structure, it's a series of maybe trillions of separate space habitats, some connected together as islands and archipelagos, and many (but not all) spinning for artificial gravity.
It isn't built as one big project, it grows organically as the population it sustains increases. A Dyson sphere can support somewhere in the realm of 50 quintillion people, and our population increases roughly 1-3% per year. Even if you account for people intentionally reproducing much more frequently and living longer, the need for a full Dyson sphere is still a really, really long way away.

I think it is plausible but unlikely that we could build a rotating space habitat (O'Neill cylinder) in an L4 or L5 solar orbit in our lifetimes. But it will largely come down to real estate prices. Once it's cheaper to build in space than to buy land on Earth, we'll start doing it. There are a lot of key technology steps we're waiting on to make it affordable. Mining the asteroid belt is a big one, but I argue that the biggest problem is launch costs. Once we have a more efficient way to get things into space, the cost of building up there drops enormously. Probably the most feasible option right now is a tethered ring space launcher like from Project Atlantis, but it still has a lot of problems. One day we will likely have a series of orbital rings around the Earth which make getting up into orbit as easy and cheap as catching a train.

But don't misunderstand me, the future of humanity is absolutely in space. It's almost certain that one day the supermajority of humanity will live in rotating space habitats rather than on planets.

62363032
>>62359800
He's trying to get to mars which is much closer and in the opposite direction. A dyson sphere the size of the sun would not be possible there isn't enough material available on our planet to construct even half of a ring.

62363034
>>62359825
We'll build it just beyond Mars' orbit, so only outer planets shitters will be caught in the shade

62363039
The solar datacenter could power everything we need. Youtube, facebook, and x. It's literally free.

62363476
>>62360306
By 2017. Musk's engineers will build a time machine.

62365296
>>62359800
t. retard

62365482
>>62359800
>market cap:2000 Trillion
>Get free passive NASDAQ boomer money for years even if it's bleeding tens of billions in revenue a year
>literal free and infinite money from boomer wallets into your company
It's not fair man...

62365560
>>62359829
only a retard with no imagination would think a dyson sphere is necessary

62366319
>>62360616
>But it will largely come down to real estate prices. Once it's cheaper to build in space than to buy land on Earth, we'll start doing it.
Makes sense, but that seems quite a way's off from now.

62367852
>>62366319
It is, but it's affected by prices in both directions: launch costs will get vastly cheaper in the coming centuries, and real estate will get incredibly expensive in desirable areas. Orbital habitats aren't directly competing with shoebox apartments in the middle of inhospitable deserts, because they have a lot of unique benefits. For starters, our expectations about living conditions will probably increase dramatically. Our current houses are enormous compared to 100 years ago, and I see no reason why houses of the future won't be 4-5x as big as they are today. Because their land area is essentially unlimited, orbital habitats let you have a BIG house, with 15-20 acres, and a close-knit community of 10-20k people. But they can also be tailored for the exact conditions that you want. You could for example have one that's perfectly tailored for use as a sanctuary for wildlife, with the climatic and geographical conditions of your choice. Or you could build a colony for your specific cultural or religious group, with the ability to physically move if you don't like your neighbours. Because orbital habitats are inherently also spaceships, they can choose to dock with whoever else they want, or go off by themselves.

62368805
>>62367852
>with the ability to physically move if you don't like your neighbours
This has always been a retarded midwit point. Spacecraft are not in long term physical close proximity in any meaningful sense

62369191
>>62368805
Except they will be, because most will logically cluster around stable or resource-rich areas, just like on Earth. L4/L5 being the obvious example, but the larger asteroids will also likely have multiple mining settlements nearby. Economies of scale do apply, so many habitats will be physically tethered to one another, or at least within trading distance. It's very cheap and easy to shuttle between them because of the lack of apparent gravity or relative speed, so I would expect individual shuttle craft on par with modern cars in terms of ubiquity and complexity.

62369249
>>62363032
its a thin shell of solar panels basically to capture the suns energy

62369638
>>62359825
Musk will sell you sunlight as a subscription service and you will be happy. Better start preparing your wallet.

>>62359829
>Technology is exponential.
We had a couple crazy centuries but we're getting close to the limits of physics as we currently know it. Technological advancement will slow down again until somebody figures out a better theory than the ones we currently have

62369643
No he isn't because he has a track record of being a lying cunt for much less ambitious things.

62369772
>>62359800
Elon mogs me so hard
FUCK
He's doing this and here I am shitposting, jacking off, and hoping my memecoins pump.

62369776
>>62369772
>shitposting, jacking off, and hoping my memecoins pump
he's doing this also

item.title: Anon, someone oneshotted a Runescape-quality MMO with Claude. — 63 replies — 30.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Anon, someone oneshotted a Runescape-quality MMO with Claude.

Open thread • Replies: 63 • Updated: 2026-06-15 11:29 UTC

OP

Anon, someone oneshotted a Runescape-quality MMO with Claude. If you're not able to get filthy rich with this tech, you are just a brainless clown.

https://worldofclaudecraft.com/

Early replies (first 3)

62362742
oldschool runescape is free

62362747
looks like shit
>If you're not able to get filthy rich with this tech, you are just a brainless clown.
sure if you can scam some subhumans but you're competing with 1 billion jeet scammers

62362749
>>62362737
Buy
An
Ad
!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62364357
People with balls played this MMO

62364390
>>62363118
it's not automatic, there's literally a mod that goes through the chat logs and bans anyone who says nigger

62364391
>>62362753
So you get to be a funko pop?

62364400
sounds gay

62364408
>>62364390
What's he get paid?

62364447
>>62362737
IMAGINE OSRS 2.0 holy

62364461
>>62362843
>>62362844
it's so over for snailcat retards

62364719
>>62362737
impressive but 7 fps isn't going to work.

62364883
>>62364719
>7FPS isn't going to work
I bet you dont have hardware acceleration on dumbie

62364978
>>62363623
the assets are free open source models and stuff so it looks like shit

The gameplay is a classic wow clone, it's shockingly similar

62365021
>>62364090
Thanks...these dumbfucks thinks it cool they reskinned something and made it gay

62365211
>>62362737
>oneshotted
100% guarantee it wasn't actually "oneshotted". Also Fable 5 got banned already, even if you could one shot, it's over now.

62365245
>>62362737
this is trash

62365483
>>62362737
Dune banned AI for a reason.

62365496
>>62365211
/loop until complete

62365514
https://github.com/levy-street/world-of-claudecraft/blob/main/src/game/interactions.ts

>if if else if if else if if else else if if if else if

it's claude code alright

62365881
>>62362737
>Runescape-quality
so it's utter unplayable dogshit?
wow AI is the future

62365955
>>62362737
>>62362753
>>62363118
Globo-Homo Of SlopCraft

62365991
>>62362737
Very interesting. Not the project though, any starter $10 RPG template is a more complete game with less bugs. It's a completely useless code base worth $0. It's only impressive to people who understand nothing about game development. But it's interesting that OP hired spam bots for promotion. Is he somehow making money off of it?

62366047
>>62362742
>>62363625
in the shitty art style
also runescape is ultrashit skinner box

62366586
>>62364357
FUCK YEAH UTANI NIGGER

62366746
>>62362737
If you rip an existing open source game yourself rather than ask Claude to do it, you get documentation to go along with it.

62366802
>>62362737
Claude made me a trading bot for LINK. I am now down 99%. Thanks, claude!

62366858
>>62366802

perfect, 2 mistakes in one go!
>/biz/ dream

62367257
>>62366746
Or you just ask claude to create documentation along with the code and you don't get in trouble with the original creators.

62367267
>>62362775
It's so funny to see all the people boasting 10000x productivity and yet nothing changed. Actually everything is worsening, again and again.

62368083
>>62362737
hard pass.

62368165
If you pay attention to this guys comment
>>62363356

Tested it. My machine is not too powerful,
and was able to make some few quests.

For me it really feels like this comment: >>62364978
>The gameplay is a classic wow clone, it's shockingly similar
I'm a bit impressed! If he did not have to do a lot by himself,
imagine how much you can achieve by what little effort!!!

>This was made in 2 days and 91% of the Max 20x plan,
>as the author stated on the Reddit thread,
>so roughly ~$200.
>Supposedly, existing free assets were used and weren't generated.

Found a youtube video, where the game runs smoothly!
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cNzI3Ll2lHA

62368174
>>62368083
I would argue because the strafe on Q and E, and you could always change it if you wanted.

62369541
>>62362775

item.title: You guys arent buying the fucking thing literally named DOT during the biggest bull market since the DOT COM bubble??? — 5 replies — 20.0/hr

I started buying at 2 and I didnt start going in with a heavier under until under 1.50.. if you liked it in 2021 and 2022 these prices are a steal

you can shill this shit as much as you want, I'll never buy anything this board shills, especially shilled hard.

Nigger.

62369949
>>62369441
"I beg you, Vitalik, look at me! Fuck you, respect me! My blockchain is useless, worthless, and deserted, but I have one just like you! I'm important, important! Please, Vitalik, look at me... I'll suck your dick..."

item.title: Elon Musk is a Trillion Dollar Cam Girl — 4 replies — 16.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Elon Musk is a Trillion Dollar Cam Girl

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-06-15 13:22 UTC

OP

Most billionaires' money comes from them running companies that do billions in profit. Buffett does 70B/year, the Google guys do 139B/year, Pony Ma of Tencent does 35B/year, etc. Elon Doesn't do this. The Musk Empire collectively LOSES money, and this is after it has had 20+ years to get it together.
What's going on here is VCs like Marc Andreesen and the Saudis, as well as similar-minded retail investors, are throwing money at Elon because he looks like the kind of alpha male they think should be running the sector. In a world where real tech companies are run by "icky" Chinese and Indian nerds, Elon looks the part better. He makes investors feel good, they give him money, so the capital bonfires continue.
https://youtu.be/mNw6sWNcvVs

Early replies (first 3)

62369808
>>62369766
sorry anon, it's over

62369828
>>62369808
Elon's career of pole dancing for right wing chud money is FAR from over.

62369920
lmao, are you going to be doing this everyday, you know there are billionaires who tried to short tesla and kept failing, but you think your shitposting is meaningful

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62369925
>>62369766
Collab with his underwear model daughter when?

item.title: Shiller PE ratio. — 7 replies — 14.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Shiller PE ratio.

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-06-15 13:04 UTC

OP

Is it just a bunch of bullshit?
Are there still big brain anons here?

Early replies (first 3)

62369579
>>62369517
>Is it just a bunch of bullshit?
No, it's just the manipulation can go harder than your expectations.

62369631
>>62369517
The higher the P/E the more future growth is priced in. It's therefor not enough to materialize this growth, because the P/E will then just normalize, and the stock kind of stay flat.. Unless future-future-growth get priced in, that you maintain the P/E while meeting the anticipated growth.

62369645
Tech has been elevated p:e forever, and its a larger and larger part of the index

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62369727
>>62369631
>>62369645
But AI has not yet proven to be profitable. All the ai companies lose money

62369786
>>62369517
>any big brain anons around
I count there are currently at least 3 full blown commie threads up right now in /biz/
What do you think?

62369803
>>62369786
commie threads meaning what? communist bullshit?

62369831
>>62369727
yep thats why we call it the future growth

item.title: I got a 20% raise yesterday. $120 --> $144k (plus $7k-$10k annual bonuses). I also just paid off my car which frees up $… — 44 replies — 12.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I got a 20% raise yesterday. $120 --> $144k (plus $7k-$10k annual bonuses). I also just paid off my car which frees up $…

Open thread • Replies: 44 • Updated: 2026-06-15 09:53 UTC

OP

I got a 20% raise yesterday. $120 --> $144k (plus $7k-$10k annual bonuses). I also just paid off my car which frees up $500/month. What should I do with the extra income?

Right now I basically break even when factoring in mortgage, gas/food, insurance, preschool costs, and 5% contribution to retirement (with employer match). I live in a moderately HCOL area, for context. I'm looking at an extra $1800 now per month post-tax. I'm thinking of increasing retirement contributions, put a couple hundred extra a month toward mortgage principal (5.625% interest rate), putting a little into kids college funds, and saving up what remains in HYSA for emergencies and home projects. Anything else I'm missing?

Early replies (first 3)

62363658
not your personal blog, faggot

62363663
go back you wagie normie niggerfaggot

62363668
Grats. Pay min on mortgage, no extra. Put extra into roth ira until maxed. Rest into roth 401k if available.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62364061
>>62363657
>Right now I basically break even
i hope your bitch is working.

62364072
>>62363745
>you can make much more especially with compounding
1965-1982 and 2000-2012 say "hi". Paying off the mortgage is a guaranteed win. Playing the market is only a win as long as line goes up.

62364118
Roth IRA 50/50 SCHD and VT

62364143
>>62363657
If you don't have an emergency fund, prioritize that. 3-6 months, but I'd aim closer to 6 with kids. Also, what a lot of people don't consider is building additional dedicated savings funds for known irregular expenses. (For example, you know you will eventually need a new roof, so start putting a little away every month now instead of needing to take out a loan in the future) You could save for a new car, computer, mattress, air conditioning unit, etc. this way. Think of expensive things you own that will eventually need to be replaced and start saving a little each month now for them so that down the line you aren't having to pay some interest to some jew to borrow his money in a lump sum.

The rest of your plan sounds good. Congrats on the raise!

62364145
>>62363674
Can you recast your mortgage? Given that your mortgage interest is greater than the risk-free rate, that would be a reasonable move. But otherwise, you should never put more into your mortgage payments than necessary because it affords you no benefit. If nothing else, you could just save the extra money in an emergency fund, to pay the mortgage in a later month if you lose your source of income.

62364224
>>62364061
The cost of daycare for two kids would basically be $60k/year where we live so there's no point in her working. We were also moving around a lot the last four years to get to my current long-term career so she didn't exactly have the opportunity to career build. She will be working once the younger one is in kindergarten.
>>62364055
Is the idea that it's good to diversify between pre-tax and post-tax contributions?
>>62364143
Yea , you get it. It's why I can't fully commit to retirement funds. We need a new driveway in the next couple years, for instance. This raise makes it possible. The bugger question is what's the ideal breakdown for allocation.
>>62364145
We actually financed from 6.99% to 5.625% back in...April I think? With no points. Literally the day before Trump starting bombing Iran. Mortgage payment was supposed to go down $300/month but property taxes were raised so it only went down $200...

62364230
>>62363657
But do you have seven figures? If not, you're still lower class according to /biz/

62364245
>>62364224
>it's good to diversify between pre-tax and post-tax contributions
Yes. Withdrawing from pre-tax accounts counts as income, and you pay income tax on it. But that income also affects things like medicare payments and so forth, so you want the ability to control how much of your retirement money is income and how much is tax free (Roth). It's also good to diversify between retirement and non-retirement accounts, so set up a regular brokerage account and contribute to it as well, you'll pay capital gains taxes but those are much lower than income taxes (0% up to $98k/yr if you're married filing jointly), and that also doesn't count as income. That'll give you something you can tap into if you or your wife need to retire early, e.g. to take care of a parent or something.

62364248
>>62364224
man you have two kids while being a wagie and having a mortgage?

no one in your entire family tree could give you enough money to be free?

and you think you were good enough to reproduce?

62364257
>>62364224
>what's the ideal breakdown for allocation.
There is no universal ideal, that's something that is specific to your situation. I've got about 60% of my money in pre-tax retirement accounts and I wish it were much lower. Unfortunately I've been ineligible to contribute to a Roth for most of my career, so the other 40% is in a couple of brokerage accounts that I'll have to pay capital gains taxes to access. If I were to do it again I'd only contribute enough to my 401k to get the employer match, then put the rest into an S&P 500 index fund in a brokerage account. If you can contribute to a Roth, then I'd split my non-401k contributions evenly between the Roth and brokerage funds. The 0% tax on Roth accounts is no joke, but not being able to tap into them until you're 59 1/5 is also no joke, meanwhile the relatively low capital gains tax rate takes a lot of the sting out of the brokerage accounts.

62364265
>>62364248
lol
spoilt brat faggot
you'd be penniless if you weren't carried by your parent's money

62364271
>>62364265
>family is on this planet since 2500 B.C
>still does not own a home

Yikes

62364274
>>62364245
And for the regular brokerage account, I take I should tie the money to something like S&P? The only reason I've shied away from that is in my college years I was one if those shitheads addicted to "active investment" and fear I'll blow thousands of dollars again if I open a new account.
>>62364248
>wage
I make $144k/year and work essentially my own hours with no boss. I have to be at work approx. 5 hours/week in the fall and spring (I'll let you guess my job) but that's it. So technically I'm a wagie but I never feel like it. Never have to ask time off for anything.
>>62364257
What happens if I tap before 59.5? Percent penalty? I used to have money in i-series bonds and it sucked not bring able to touch them for however long the maturity date was.

62364288
>>62364271
you have no valuable advice to offer anyone. you live in a sheltered bubble

62364289
>>62364271
OP here, yea, my family is full of financially retarded people. My dad proudly jokes that his Native American name is "Spends With Fury." And because my parents being broke led to me getting a full tuition "scholarship" to college, he feels totally justified in his actions. He tells me he plans to spend every cent of his money before he dies.

I'm doing my best to set my kids up better but inflation and rising housing costs didn't help. I have a buddy whose grandparents and parents set him up nicely. He bought a $3 million home in DC recently, all cash, is spending $750k on renovations, and doesn't even work. Must be nice. I've made my own life nearly as nice as circumstances could have allowed.

62364313
>>62364224
Here's what I would do:
>Emergency fund first, get it to 3 months asap since you have other big known expenses coming up. After that, maybe throw $100 or $200 a month into it to keep it growing while you're doing other things with your money. I really think with kids the ultimate goal should be 6 months.
>You're supposed to have roughly 1x your salary saved for retirement by 30, which you do. What I would do is increase your 401k contributions by 1% annually to make sure you keep on track until you get to about 10%. So bump it up to 6% after this raise. Open a roth IRA if you don't have an IRA already and max that out. Max out an HSA if you have one.
>Put a couple hundred into your kids' college funds
>Look at prices for new driveways in your area and start putting away monthly to pay for it. You could probably push the replacement out a few years if you need to. I mean, it's just a bunch of concrete you drive on top of, it's doesn't have to be pristine. It's not an emergency if it's got a bunch of cracks in it.
>Then increase mortgage payments

Also, look for ways to save money. $144k/year after taxes should leave you about $100k. Subtracting mortgage and the $1800/month you have freed up means you're spending roughly $3300/month on non-mortgage expenses. I'm not saying you need to eat beans for every meal or skip vacations or stop enjoying your hobbies, but take a look at the stuff you buy and think about if you really need to be spending money on that. If you do, fine, but it's worth a look to see if you can free up extra money.
You'll probably be getting more pay raises and your wife will start working in the future too so even if you feel stretched thin now, don't stress about it, you're on the right track and things will get easier.

>I should tie the money to something like S&P?
Yeah, that or total stock market, total world stock market. Any broad index fund you like and then don't touch it.

62364320
>>62364315
are you seriously coping about being a trust fund kid so hard you have memes saved to your computer to use when people point this out online

62364419
>>62364289
Have you considered aggressively investing in shitcoins?

62364493
>>62364419
Unironically the only reason I own my house is Bitcoin. Beginning in 2022 I started DCAing Bitcoin and sold in December 2024 for a $30k gain. Managed to have just enough for a 20% down payment for my house.
>>62364313
Yes I could be tighter with money. With dependent care and retirement and taxes all taken out of my paycheck, my estimated monthly take home with the raise is $8,050. Minus mortgage and the $1,800 extra, I'm at $3,067.

Let's say $500/month for utilities (sometimes more), $75/month for phone plan, $500/month for groceries, $200/month for gas, $300/month on medical expenses (wife is Type 1), $100/month on car insurance, and $200/month toward old medical debt.

That leaves about $1,200/month extra. Some of that goes toward smaller projects (like I had to get a tree guy to get fungus off our front yard tree). But I know we also probably spend too much on eating out (probably a few hundred a month) and other odds and ends.

62364668
>>62364257
Why didnt you backdoor roth? Also roth conversions can be accessed after 5 years and there are 72t sepps to access money penalty free before 59.5. Maybe hold off on giving advice unless you k ow what youre talking about, but you are on the right track in realizing too much pretax becomes an issue. 500k/person youre supporting is where you might start to get in trouble if you dont begin to draw down soon and dont have deduction tricks up your sleeve.

62364705
>>62364668
Also roth contributions are accessible anytime penalty free. As far as brokerages go, a married couple gets almost 100k tax free in qualified dividends or ltcgs, roc isnt taxed at all, and you can gain harvest. Im not evwn going to begin to get into deduction hacks. All yall need some education.

62364709
>>62364705
**tax free if there is no other income exceeding deductions

62364805
>>62364274
>my wife can't work because I work so much
>I have to be at work approx. 5 hours/week
So which is it?

62364813
>>62364805
Are you retarded? When did I say my wife can't work because I work so much?

62364824
>>62364813
When you said childcare is $60k/yr if she got a job. Why tf can't she work if you're home all the time? Do you hate your children? So you have to stay far away from them despite not working?

Whole thread being a LARP confirmed.

62364851
>>62364824
If you need it spelled out, I work in academia. My "required" time in office is teaching. But I also still research and write scholarly pieces. But I enjoy doing that and get to write about whatever I want so it doesn't feel like work. But I cannot write and also raise kids full time. I wasn't trying to imply that I literally only work 5 hours a week.

62365713
>>62364668
>Why didnt you backdoor roth?
The same factors that make me ineligible to contribute to a roth also makes roth conversions uneconomical.

62369349
>>62363674
>I just hate being in debt
Poorfag mentality. You need to grow up and recognize good vs bad debt.

62365840

62365850

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62365852

62365855
>>62365850
unfortunately, to achieve fast finality they would have to cap the validator set

62365892
>>62365855
Yes, its decenrralized.

62365899
The best incentive is no incentive…incentivized blockchains will not make it long term

62365925
>>62365850
Speed has very little to do with the current situation

62366074
What does crypto have going for it atm? Banking on the whole 'cycle' narrative is retarded and feels scammy to say the least. The market is too volatile and openly manipulated. They aren't even trying to hide it anymore. Is the crypto space cooked for real or what?

62366099
>>62366074
Tokenization of real world assets is what I’m excited about….but I’m a land owner

62366122
>>62365835
Don't worry, the Clarity Act will be the wave that lifts all ships! Hang tight!

62366127
>>62366099
I'm a bit of a retard, but what does that mean? I saw people mentioning RWAs for years now and I never thought much of it. It seems like it still hasn't taken off yet?

62366404
>>62365835
I've been holding eth since 2015, sold a couple weeks back. Fuck this gay shit.

62366649
>>62366127
RWAs is just another crypto buzzword. It’s part of a long list of other useless buzzwords that have been spammed here for years. No one cares about crypto ai, web3, crypto gaming, depin, privacy, rwas, etc. it’s all bullshit pushed on by scammers and grifters

62366825
>>62366127
There are trillions of dollars worth of illiquid assets out there (land, gold, diamonds, art, wine, etc.)
Tokenization of rwa will let people tokenize their asset, borrow a stablecoin against it, park it somewhere and collect 4-8% interest
Most normies like the retard above do not understand this because most of them do not own any assets (like land).
Tokenization will make me a shit ton of money, and this is also why it will pump crypto that is well positioned to tokenize assets (xrp)
Plan accordingly

62366836
>>62366127
And it hasn’t taken off because it hasn’t been legal to do so, but the passage of the clarity act will remedy this…also why banks are seething

62366913
>>62365835
>What if ETH actually doesn't recover though
Swap what value you have left into XMR

62366917
>>62365835
Wait, what? Why would it go back to ATH again?

62367060
>>62365835
i cry

62369489
>>62366404
But dumping ETH after diamond-handing for a decade is peak exit liquidity behavior. Hope you rotated into BTC and parked it on Babylon where you have full custody, way better than rage-jeeting after surviving all those cycles.

item.title: So when is SPCX going to crash? — 20 replies — 5.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
So when is SPCX going to crash?

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-06-15 10:02 UTC

OP

> Next FOMC meeting
> Next earnings date
> Right when it gets into the Nasdaq100
> ?
There is no way that a company priced in hopium that actually loses money is not going to crash like a shitcoin at some point.

Early replies (first 3)

62366003
>>62365854
When is TSLA going to crash?

Musk can string out the SPCX hopium even longer than the TSLA hopium, possibly for decades.

62366023
>>62365854
I expect that every test launch of a SuperHeavy/Starship will create an options trader's dream.
* rocket blows up, stock price plunges
* test goes perfectly, stock price zooms

So, just trade straddles starting a day or two before every big event.

Ever seen Boeing's stock price after a crash? One plane goes down, whole stock drops. Never mind that it's usually either defective maintenance or the pilots' fault.

62366153
>>62365854
Look at this as you'd look at a shitcoin TGE.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62366334
>>62366153
Shitcoins crash so fucking hard when they do.

62366341
>>62366003
Tesla actually makes money, Spacex is a bunch of AI space datacenters dream bullshit. Only Starlink makes any money in it.

62366378
It will never be below 135. Only low iq brainlests that don’t understand how important mass/cost to orbit is and how important orbital refueling is think it like mega epic overvalued

Oh and SpaceX currently has no heavy lift competition. Only Amazon is in the field and their rocket exploded recently, they are years behind. Other competitors like rklb and fly can barely do small lift. Rklb is still working on neutron to compete with falcon on medium lift. SpaceX is a monopoly currently that will unlock the next frontier. But yeah you’re right, there is nothing to do in space and its just going to crash

62366436
>>62366378
Nigga once the money tap breaks down the market gets fucking fair very fucking quick.
Tesla has done -75% in the past.

62366443
>>62366003
Tesla makes cars, ai, robots, charging stations, solar panels, power batteries and electrical grid technology.
It wont.

62366451
>>62366436
Okay? It has also do 25000% growth since 2013. Your rebuttal?

62366518
>>62366451
The point is to figure out when it is going to crash to buy very low. If you want to get it now that is trading at infinite pe ratio go ahead.

62366779
>>62365854
Even if its not vaporware, these projects all take decades. There will be multiple overall market crashes/correctioba before they get anything done. Being a single stock, its gonna crash more often then the whole market. 100% chance of a dump right before any big got contracts are announces too so insiders can load up.

62366790
When all of Reddit and biz thinks something’s gonna crash, it’s probably gonna moon

Not in the stock but am now tempted for a short term position

62367082
>>62365854
The index inclusion narrative is getting frontrun. SPCX will go 30% up from its IPO price and go down from there

62367245
>>62365854
Just wait for another rocket explosion.
Buy the dip.
This shit is so easy.

62369275
>>62366378
Sending shit to space doesn't rely on the value of stocks. It's going to trade up and down. It will probably dump soon when people are allowed to sell and/or when the earnings come in.

Buy when it comes down. If they can actually successfully make data centers in space the value will moon.

62369319
>>62366790
This. I bought hundreds of shares based solely on Reddit tears.

62369336
>>62365854
i give it a couple of weeks

62369347
Retail are idiots that buy no matter what because they have no hope of a future otherwise. Fundamentals literally don't matter anymore, really they haven't since 2023

62369355
the only way it crashes is if the 10 million starlink users stop using it and that doesn't sound plausible, i mean when califortards started burning teslas, the stock went down but came back up because it was a nothing burger local skirmish, it turns out politics and business don't mix, don't let the leftard musk hate shilling inform your financial decisions, most of them are delusional hypocrites, very few of them--in fact this proves there's very few of them--have any practical convictions

62369365
>>62365854
Space X is hopeless endeavor since moment when Elon decided land on tower!

13 or so flights and not even Orbit?
Once lucky landing?
Since nothing ..except blowing up money!

Meantime Blue Origin despite latest seatback Land on second try and deploy satellite?

X is just coupium !

Elon not going even to Moon anywhere beyond 2050!

It is all hype and money scam !
You should understand that by now!

item.title: WTF — 14 replies — 4.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
WTF

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-06-15 10:16 UTC

OP

>try day trading
>lose
>try holding spot
>lose
>try trade with trend
>lose
>try trade against trend
>lose
>try day trading
>lose
>try any trading method
>lose
wtf i should do bros i dont want to return wageslaving still has 30k capital left.

Early replies (first 3)

62366888
>>62366886
You clearly haven't lost enough, keep going.

62366893
You should be wageslaving until you have either 400k in investments or enough to make bare minimum 6k a month in interest. You've fucked up, OP

62366920
>>62366886
Spent 60 hours a week studying trading strategies for at least a year before you put in real money.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62366946
there's numbers are probability of a loss based on how long you hold a stock

just holding for one year, you have 75% odds that you'll be up

62366985
>>62366886
Bro Im going to give some advice. Put that 30k on Topuria to win tonight for an easy 20%
Or better yet put that 30k on Sean O'Malley to win tonight first for an easy 20%. and then use that 36k on Topuria.
If you dont do this, im coming back to this thread later to laugh at you

62367071
>>62366886
Keep going you’re bound to make it

62367099
your day-trading sounds more like gambling
search for investment strategy instead!
>work in the meantime

62367210
>>62366985
>put that 30k on Sean O'Malley to win tonight first for an easy 20%. and then use that 36k on Topuria.
>win 10k
>put 30k on both o'malley and topuria
>win 13k
youre a true biztard kek

62367235
>>62366886
You have 100% failure rate, that's impressive. Just do the opposite of what you wanted to do.

62367264
>>62367210
recheck ur math retard

62367330
>>62366886
bro just don't be a fucking loser it's that simple.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1gZLNh7gRbQ

62369375
>>62367264
nigger
>30k on o'malley turns into 36k
>36k on topuria turns into 40k
>30k to 40k you won 10k net
>30k on both omalley and topuria turns into 43k
>30k to 43k you won 13k net
also faggot your bitch ass nigger got tko'd

62369379

62369386
>>62366886
Maybe you should start use your brain instead play in Casino (NYSE/bitcoin)

item.title: 100% VA disability is 4k a month tax free — 13 replies — 3.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
100% VA disability is 4k a month tax free

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-06-15 09:54 UTC

OP

In the US you would need a NW of about 2 mil to have the same withdrawal power

Early replies (first 3)

62367712
>>62367693
most of those guys did 10-20 years active duty in a bullshit job that nobody wants to do and then spent years and thousands of dollars fighting to get their disability.

I am not jealous of what they had to do to get that money.

62367716
>>62367712
bullshit

some of these guys are 35 y/o and already living the life in Pattaya

62367718
i know it because i met a nigger on Grindr when i was in Pattaya and he told me how he retired at 33 y/o

dude had a massive dick could not even put it inside me so i just sucked it

he was an alcoholic btw, he said he had ptsd but it's hard to believe it

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62367725
>>62367712
WW2 and Korean vets are mostly gone
Vietnam draftees should get gibs because it was horseshit they had to go to the jungle
No additional exceptions and gibs should have been limited in all the towelhead wars nobody was forced to fight

62367738
>>62367712
mcdonalds is a bullshit job no one wants to do and they dont get nearly the same benefits so how does that matter

62367745
>>62367693
best i could do was $20.50/hr
after taxes i barely cleared 2k a month
but yea lets give twice that to people to do nothing, forever, because they were used by the MIC.
god i only wish i could live to see the govt collapse.

62367748
>>62367712
also there hasnt been a real war in ages like the other guy said.. blowing up poor muslim farmers that are in flip flops with ak47s isnt hard or that tough really..

if you fought a real enemy and had to see some shit i guess

62367878
>>62367716
Kek.
>>62367718
Yeah this city is magnet for the worst people in Thailand I like. I won't mention the good places online.

62368252
>>62367718
based fag knower

62368265
>>62367693
Are you the same guy thats posting about va disability every single week and cross posting on /pol/ about it? You're mentally ill

62368274
i get this and im poor

62368282
Low class. Nice. No ownership. Nice.

62369351
>>62367745
>but yea lets give twice that to people to do nothing, forever, because they were used by the MIC.
yes
you can say that the modern military is retarded and pointless at best and the hands of globalist tyrants at worst but any political system which doesn't reward its soldiers handsomely for their service will cease to exist from lack of an army
realistically modern welfare queen soldiers are under compensated if anything where they need to eat shit for years to still get claims denied because having a 105mm going off next to you "isn't service related" while ancient soldiers were the only ones with the right to vote, got free colonial land, or could earn minor titles of nobility

item.title: WE ARE THE OILMARINES — 17 replies — 3.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
WE ARE THE OILMARINES

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-06-15 08:25 UTC

OP

AND WE ARE NEVER SELLING
1k eoy

Early replies (first 3)

62361102
>>62361092
kek

62361136
My real life would have unironically began if oil pumped beyond $200. Alas.

62361150
>>62361136
I was never possible. The recession that would have started at 150 would have immediately lowered the price again. Also reserve releases, flexible demand, increase of production by usa and russia, the pipeline of the saudis and more factors were always there. I told oilschizos that but they wouldnt believe me.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62361168
AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA

62361804
Trump.. please don't take the deal.. My oil bags..

62361809
>>62361092
No. The underlying machinery would function as is now even if it were $3 a barrel.

Oil pricing is literally about how much blood you can squeeze from the peasants

62361831
>>62361092
>>62361168
>He bought dead dinosaur blood

62364177
Gulf oil output will take years to recover, and the fraud and accounting gymnastics done to keep the price low will have to be paid for. $150 EOY

62364518
>>62364177
>>62361150

62367392
>>62361092
HODL ON MARINES

62367941
>>62361092
is there a reddit community? i need a good laugh.

62367948
>>62361092
All marines are oil marines.

62367951
>>62367941
You're posting in one.

62367975
>>62361150
oil wouldn't have recessed the world at 150 , lol oil at 150 when adjusted for inflation is still under what is was by 46 dollars in 2007

62369178
>>62367975
Kek no inflation was maybe 100% since then not 220%. And yes it would have recessed the world, as it did back then. Because you can't just x3 oil and not do it. Back then the absolute numbers were higher, but the world was used to it. Now the world is used to cheap oil.
